tasks.title: Add meaningful create-form placeholders
tasks.objective: Improve the cohort request form with realistic placeholder and example text that helps creators understand what each field expects.
authority: Use meaningful Cohort15-specific examples instead of generic lorem ipsum.
Keep examples consistent with current validation rules and meeting-link provider limits.
Do not change domain behavior unless a placeholder reveals a missing validation issue.
acceptance_criteria: Important create-form text fields render useful, realistic placeholder or helper examples.
Examples clarify expected content without replacing labels or submitted values.
Examples do not expose or suggest private links in public-safe fields.
Focused tests or snapshots assert that representative placeholders render.
verification: Run `npm run check` before issue closeout.
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when form guidance is clear and verified.

tasks.title: Support familiar local image selection
tasks.objective: Replace or augment the custom event image input with a standard local file-picker interaction that stores or references the selected image safely.
authority: Implement the smallest dependency-free local file-picker path compatible with the current app.
Preserve default image behavior when no custom image is selected.
Validate image type/size enough to avoid accepting arbitrary unsafe content.
Do not introduce third-party upload or cloud storage dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: The create form presents a recognizable click-to-choose image input.
Submitting a valid local image associates that image with the created cohort.
Feed/detail/dashboard image rendering still works for default and custom images.
Invalid or unsupported uploads fail clearly without creating a broken cohort.
Tests cover the selected local image path and default fallback.
verification: Run `npm run check` before issue closeout.
Browser smoke test `/cohorts/new` if UI changes are implemented.
tasks.area: create form, event images, asset handling
tasks.stop_condition: Stop when image selection uses a familiar local file-picker interaction and rendering remains verified.
